back_translation: In those days, John the Baptist appeared and proclaimed in the wilderness of the Judean region.
And he said, 'Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has drawn near.'
This is the one who was spoken about by Isaiah the prophet, saying: 'A voice of one crying in the wilderness, "Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight His paths."'
As for John, he was clothed with a garment made from camel's hair, and had a belt made of animal leather fastened around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
At that time, the people of Jerusalem and the people of the Judean region in general, together with people throughout the whole Jordan River basin, streamed out to him.
They confessed their sins and received baptism from him in the Jordan River.
When he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to receive his baptism, he said to them, 'You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?'
'And do not think in your hearts, "We have Abraham as our father," for I tell you all that God is able to raise up children of Abraham out of these stones.'
'Now the axe is already poised at the base of the trees; therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.'
'I baptize you with water for repentance, but the one who will come after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not worthy even to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.'
'The winnowing fork is already in His hand; He will thoroughly cleanse His threshing floor and gather His wheat into the barn, but He will burn up the chaff with fire that never goes out.'
At that time, Jesus came from the region of Galilee to the Jordan River to receive baptism from John.
But John humbly protested to Him, saying, 'It is rather I who should receive baptism from You, and yet do You come to me?'
Jesus answered him, 'Allow it to be so at this time, for it is fitting for us both to accomplish all righteousness completely.' Then John allowed Him to receive baptism.
When Jesus had received baptism, He went up out of the water immediately; and behold, the heavens opened, and He beheld the Spirit of God descending like a dove, alighting upon Him.
And behold, a voice from heaven declared, 'This is My beloved Son; I am well pleased with this One exceedingly.'
